sections.paragraph:
(once תֹּום Pro 10:9) followed by Makk. תָּס־, with suffix תֻּמִּי (from the root תָּמַם), m. integrity, wholeness.

(1) of number and measure, fulness, Isa 47:9, כְּתֻמָּם “in full measure.”

(2) of fortune, welfare, safety, prosperity, i.q. שָׁלֹום. Job 21:23, בְּעֶצֶם תֻּמֹּו “in his very prosperity;” Psa 41:13.

(3) integrity of mind, innocence. תָּס־לֵבָב integrity of heart, Gen 20:5, Gen 20:6הָלַךְ בְּתֹם Pro 10:9, and בְּתֻמֹּו Psa 26:1, to live uprightly. Used of simplicity of mind, which is opposed to mischief and ill-design, 1Ki 22:34, “he drew a bow לְתֻמֹּו in his simplicity,” without any evil purpose; 2Sa 15:11, הֹלְכִים לְתֻמָּם “going (with him) with a simple mind” (not conscious of an evil design).

(4) pl. תֻּמִּים [Thummim], truth (LXX. ἀλήθεια), see אוּרִים No. 1, b .
